article.title: Uber - Sr. Security Investigator - L5A
article.content:
I have a BPS/Phone screen scheduled currently. The role itself is centered around cyber incident response/investigations so was surprised to see a live coding assessment. This is certainly not my strong suit, but I can do basic things in python. 

Anyone have any insight here on what to expect? It appears it will only be half of the interview, so less coding than a general SWE.. but still surprising given the role in my opinion. 

Thanks all!

post.content: If coding isn't your favorite, try focusing on problem-solving instead of just syntax. I've been using PracHub, and the questions are spot-on with what recruiters usually ask. Be sure to review basic data structures and algorithms. It'll be useful even if the role involves more investigation. Good luck!
